mission.title: Install and Configure ORAS on Kubernetes
mission.description: ORAS (OCI Registry As Storage) is a CLI tool and Go library for pushing and pulling OCI artifacts to and from OCI-compliant registries. It enables using container registries to store any type of artifact, extending beyond container images.

steps.title: Install the ORAS CLI
steps.description:
Download and install the ORAS CLI binary from the official release artifacts. The example below targets Linux amd64. For other platforms, replace the `linux_amd64` suffix with `linux_arm64`, `darwin_arm64` (Apple silicon) or `darwin_amd64` (Intel Mac).
```bash
VERSION="1.3.2"
curl -LO "https://github.com/oras-project/oras/releases/download/v${VERSION}/oras_${VERSION}_linux_amd64.tar.gz"
mkdir -p oras-install/
tar -zxf oras_${VERSION}_linux_amd64.tar.gz -C oras-install/
sudo chown root:0 oras-install/oras
sudo mv oras-install/oras /usr/local/bin/
rm -rf oras_${VERSION}_linux_amd64.tar.gz oras-install/
```
Alternative installation methods:
```bash
# Homebrew (macOS/Linux)
brew install oras

# Snap (Linux) -- classic confinement is required
snap install oras --classic

# Go 1.24+
go install oras.land/oras/cmd/oras@v1.3.0
```
> Note: package managers such as Homebrew, Snap, WinGet and Nix may lag behind the latest GitHub release. Use the release artifacts above to guarantee version 1.3.2.

steps.title: Verify the ORAS installation
steps.description:
Confirm the CLI is on your PATH and reports the expected version. For production use, also verify the downloaded binary against the published checksums and signatures (see https://oras.land/docs/how_to_guides/verifying_binaries).
```bash
oras version
```
Expected output (Go version, OS/Arch and commit will vary):
```
Version:        1.3.2
Go version:     go1.26.2
OS/Arch:        linux/amd64
Git commit:     fe425992fdfdf300a1cfb380bc4271b3e1a3d3db
Git tree state: clean
```

steps.title: Push an artifact to a registry
steps.description:
Use ORAS to push a file as an OCI artifact to a container registry. Setting an explicit artifact type makes the artifact easier to discover later.
```bash
echo "Hello ORAS" > artifact.txt
oras push localhost:5000/myartifact:v1 --artifact-type application/vnd.acme.artifact.v1 artifact.txt:text/plain
```

steps.title: Pull an artifact from a registry
steps.description:
Use ORAS to pull an OCI artifact back from the registry.
```bash
mkdir output
cd output
oras pull localhost:5000/myartifact:v1
cat artifact.txt
```

steps.title: Use ORAS in a Kubernetes Job
steps.description:
Create a Kubernetes Job that uses ORAS to pull artifacts as part of a workflow.
```bash
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: oras-pull-job
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: oras
        image: ghcr.io/oras-project/oras:v1.3.2
        command: ["oras"]
        args: ["pull", "registry.example.com/myartifact:v1", "-o", "/output"]
        volumeMounts:
        - name: output
          mountPath: /output
      volumes:
      - name: output
        emptyDir: {}
      restartPolicy: Never
EOF
```

steps.title: Verify the Kubernetes Job completed
steps.description:
Confirm the Job ran to completion and inspect its logs for the ORAS pull output.
```bash
kubectl get job oras-pull-job
kubectl logs job/oras-pull-job
```
A successful run shows the Job's COMPLETIONS as 1/1. If the Job failed, the logs surface the registry or authentication error.

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the ORAS CLI available for pushing and pulling OCI artifacts, with `oras version` reporting 1.3.2. ORAS can also be used within Kubernetes Jobs to manage artifact distribution as part of CI/CD pipelines.
codeSnippets:
VERSION="1.3.2"
curl -LO "https://github.com/oras-project/oras/releases/download/v${VERSION}/oras_${VERSION}_linux_amd64.tar.gz"
mkdir -p oras-install/
tar -zxf oras_${VERSION}_linux_amd64.tar.gz -C oras-install/
sudo chown root:0 oras-install/oras
sudo mv oras-install/oras /usr/local/bin/

uninstall.title: Remove the ORAS binary
uninstall.description: Delete the ORAS binary from /usr/local/bin/ (`sudo rm /usr/local/bin/oras`) or uninstall via your package manager (`brew uninstall oras` or `snap remove oras`).

uninstall.title: Clean up artifacts
uninstall.description: Delete any OCI artifacts pushed to registries if no longer needed.

upgrade.title: Download the latest ORAS release
upgrade.description: Re-run the release-artifact install step with an updated VERSION value, download the latest binary from the GitHub releases page, or run `brew upgrade oras`. Confirm the result with `oras version`.

troubleshooting.title: Push fails with authentication error
troubleshooting.description:
Login to the registry before pushing.
```bash
oras login registry.example.com
```

troubleshooting.title: Pull fails with not found error
troubleshooting.description:
Verify the artifact reference (registry/repository:tag) is correct and exists in the registry.
```bash
oras repo tags registry.example.com/myartifact
oras manifest fetch registry.example.com/myartifact:v1
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have kubectl installed. An OCI-compliant container registry is needed for pushing and pulling artifacts.
